Now I understand! The `isRead()` method at line 583-591 compares the read date with the last request time. A session is considered unread if the last request time is after the read date. This means every time a new request is made, the session becomes unread until marked as read again.

The issue is that when the user has a chat open and interacts with it, the session becomes unread immediately. The comment from @joshspicer suggests we should never mark an open chat as unread.

Excellent! The analysis is complete. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Summary

**Bug:** Every chat interaction shows "1 unread" in the Agents control, causing the title indicator to flash even when the user is actively viewing the chat.

**Root Cause:** The `isRead()` method in `AgentSessionsModel` only compares timestamps to determine if a session is read. It doesn't check if the session is currently open in a chat widget, so active interactions trigger unread indicators.

**Proposed Fix:*
